methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Keen Technologies, a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by John Carmack, has raised $20M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of nine private robotics firms and one public peer, though narrow stage and tier alignment limited full triangulation.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable approach, using the $20M raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage transitions in venture capital. This yields an implied valuation of $50M. Peer-median analysis was not applicable; while eleven same-sector robotics companies exist, fewer than three qualified within adjacent-stage or similar-valuation-tier filters, making a narrow peer set unreliable. A broad-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given the stage-specific nature of valuation.

The triangulated range is $42M to $57M, with a median of $50M, derived from the single applicable method. This analysis carries material uncertainty around comparable company selection—stage-appropriate peers in frontier robotics remain sparse—and reflects only the raised capital proxy rather than revenue, growth rate, or technology differentiation metrics. The $50M median should be treated as indicative pending access to wider peer financials or operational data that would support revenue or SaaS multiple approaches.
